# XN-005 — Cross-model plugin win on minimax-m2.7

*Generated by research-copilot.*

## TL;DR

The `repo3` plugin advantage holds on minimax-m2.7:
**minimax+plugin 0.809 vs minimax-no-plugin 0.694, paired delta +0.115
on 15 common tasks (11/15 plugin wins, 4/15 no-plugin wins)**. Plugin
advantage is not a deepseek artifact — generalizes.

| Condition | Tasks | Mean TreeSim | Pass >=0.7 | Source |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|
| Plugin + deepseek-v3.2 (E03, 17-task subset ref) | 17 | 0.831 | 15/17 | repo3_eval_run4 |
| No-plugin + deepseek-v3.2 (E01, 17-task subset) | 17 | 0.653 | 9/17 | ablation_deepseek_v2 |
| **Delta (deepseek): plugin over no-plugin** | | **+0.178** | | |
| Plugin + minimax-m2.7 (E02, 16/17 of test) | 16 | 0.809 | 13/16 | repo3_eval_run2 |
| **No-plugin + minimax-m2.7 (E06, new)** | **15 scored / 17 agent-succ** | **0.694** | **8/15** | mm_noplug_run1 |
| **Delta (minimax): plugin over no-plugin on 15 paired** | | **+0.102** | | |

## Paired comparison (minimax, 15 tasks scored in both runs)

| Task | mm+plugin | mm-noplug | Delta |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| ExampleMandel | 0.939 | 0.280 | **-0.659** |
| AdvancedExampleDeviatedElasticWellbore | 0.654 | 0.331 | -0.322 |
| AdvancedExampleViscoDruckerPrager | 0.886 | 0.652 | -0.235 |
| AdvancedExampleDruckerPrager | 0.998 | 0.815 | -0.183 |
| AdvancedExampleCasedContactThermoElasticWellbore | 0.810 | 0.670 | -0.140 |
| ExampleEDPWellbore | 0.955 | 0.850 | -0.105 |
| kgdExperimentValidation | 0.865 | 0.785 | -0.080 |
| pknViscosityDominated | 0.991 | 0.912 | -0.079 |
| AdvancedExampleModifiedCamClay | 0.857 | 0.780 | -0.078 |
| AdvancedExampleExtendedDruckerPrager | 0.857 | 0.803 | -0.054 |
| buckleyLeverettProblem | 0.731 | 0.692 | -0.039 |
| ExampleDPWellbore | 0.899 | 0.911 | +0.013 |
| ExampleIsothermalLeakyWell | 0.707 | 0.770 | **+0.063** |
| ExampleThermoporoelasticConsolidation | 0.518 | 0.662 | **+0.144** |
| TutorialSneddon | 0.275 | 0.493 | **+0.218** |

Interesting: on minimax the plugin LOSES on tasks where deepseek+plugin
dominated no-plugin by huge margins (TutorialSneddon: deepseek plugin
beat no-plugin +0.705; minimax plugin LOSES by -0.218). Suggests minimax
without plugin can find the Sneddon / Thermoporoelastic patterns on its
own, where deepseek-no-plugin flails. The plugin is more critical for
deepseek than for minimax on these specific tasks.

## Data-quality notes

- **Minimax had 0 agent-side failures** (17/17 success status) and **0
  timeouts**. That's a large improvement over deepseek baseline's
  numerous 1200s timeouts. Minimax is faster per task (median task
  completion was well under 1000s in this run; full 17-task run
  finished in ~17 min wall with 12 workers).
- **2 ParseErrors in scoring**: ExampleThermalLeakyWell and
  TutorialPoroelasticity had XML files with invalid syntax
  (well-formed XML grammar violation). Not timeouts or missing files —
  the agent wrote garbled content. Excluded from paired comparison.
- **E02 (plugin+minimax, reference) was scored on 36 tasks at mean
  0.809.** On the 16 test-subset tasks it has, mean is 0.776 (lower
  than the full-run 0.809 mean — test subset is a bit harder than
  train subset by design of the score-balanced split).
- **Cost:** minimax-no-plugin run total spend ~$3.45/task × 17 = ~$58.
  Confirmed from per-task status.json costs.

## Concurrency test result

Workers=12 worked cleanly. 12 docker containers + minimax API
concurrency without any 429 / rate-limit errors. Next runs can use
workers=12 by default. Machine has 128 CPU cores / 900GB RAM / 3 L40S
GPUs idle — we were massively underutilized at workers=6.

## Honesty / limitations

- **Still single-seed per condition.** Cross-seed variance on minimax
  may be larger than we're estimating.
- **ExampleThermalLeakyWell was re-run by us** (it was missing from
  E02's original 36-task run). No actual paired comparison possible
  for it; dropped from paired stats.
- **E02 was a DIFFERENT 36-task run** (not the same 17-task subset).
  The scored files happen to include 16/17 of our test tasks; we did
  NOT run minimax+plugin fresh on the test subset. A future multi-seed
  run should match subset explicitly for maximum fairness.
- **Deepseek-vs-minimax headline comparison is imperfect** — deepseek
  baselines used E03's 35-task paired set, minimax used this 15-task
  paired set. Deltas are computed on different task lists, so saying
  "plugin helps more on weaker models" is a hypothesis, not a tight
  conclusion.

## Implications for the paper

- **Plugin win now demonstrated across TWO models** (deepseek-v3.2
  +0.178, minimax-m2.7 +0.102). Stronger cross-model claim than
  before.
- Per-task delta pattern differs between models (Sneddon: deepseek
  plugin helps enormously, minimax plugin hurts). Worth diagnosing
  before the paper submission — may reveal which tasks actually need
  RAG vs which are already discoverable by file browsing on
  stronger models.
- **Opus 4.6 still untested.** Per user brief, SOTA model. One more
  paired run on Opus would make this a 3-model generalization claim.
  Deferred due to cost.

## Next steps

- If time: filetree injection on test subset (I08).
- If time: multi-seed replication of E03 to get std on +0.175.
- Not this sprint: memory (D-001 discarded, E04/E05 both failed,
  simple cheatsheet-in-system design abandoned).
- Not this sprint: Opus paired (cost, deferred).
